channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Three of Trump's most controversial Cabinet picks set for hearings
item.description: More hearings have been scheduled for Trump’s Cabinet nominees, marking a significant week ahead for some of his more controversial picks.
item.dc:creator: Elina Shirazi, NewsNation

item.title: Gabbard's path through Intelligence Committee narrows ahead of key hearing
item.description: Republicans are cautioning that Tulsi Gabbard’s path to confirmation to lead the U.S. Intelligence apparatus is narrowing as she seemingly has trouble winning over key GOP senators. Gabbard, along with Robert F. Kennedy Jr.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Senate tees up more confirmations as House GOP talks Trump agenda
item.description: (The Hill) — The Senate is set to churn through more of President Donald Trump’s cabinet nominees this week, with a handful of his controversial picks set to field questions during their confirmation hearings. Tulsi Gabbard, Trump’s pick to be director of national intelligence; Robert F. Kennedy Jr.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Several bills filed to weaken vaccine mandates as more Texas families opt out of immunizations
item.description: Emboldened by Robert F. Kennedy Jr’s nomination and disdain for pandemic-era mandates, skeptics are pushing for bills to make it easier to opt out of vaccines.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Terri Langford And Dan Keemahill, KPRC 2

item.title: Tennessee lawmaker reintroduces bill to allow veterans, retired cops to work as School Resource Officers
item.description: A Tennessee state lawmaker reintroduced legislation that would allow veterans and retired law enforcement officers to be hired as School Resource Officers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Cali Rep. Chu says 'wildfires have no political affiliations' after Trump floated conditions for federal aid
item.description: Rep. Judy Chu said she does not believe conditions should be placed on federal aid for wildfires sweeping through Southern California, after President Donald Trump suggested such.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Intelligence sharing by the US and its allies has saved lives. Trump could test those ties
item.description: Current and former European and U.S. officials have raised concerns about some of President Donald Trump’s picks for top intelligence posts.
item.dc:creator: Emma Burrows And David Klepper, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: After raucous first week in office, Donald Trump to keep his foot on the gas
item.description: Following a busy first week of his second term, President Donald Trump has a number of key policies to advance as week two begins.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump’s Palestinian refugee idea falls flat with Arab allies and confounds a Republican senator
item.description: President Donald Trump’s push to have Egypt and Jordan take in large numbers of Palestinian refugees from besieged Gaza has fallen flat with both countries' governments and perplexed a congressional ally.
item.dc:creator: Will Weissert, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Sen. Marshall 'absolutely' comfortable with Trump’s purge of inspectors general
item.description: Sen. Roger Marshall (R-Kan.) suggested that, like Reagan, Trump could potentially rehire some of the watchdog officials after cleaning house at the agencies.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: White House says Colombia agrees to take deported migrants after Trump tariff showdown
item.description: The White House is claiming victory in a showdown with Colombia over accepting flights of deported migrants from the U.S., hours after President Donald Trump threatened steep tariffs on imports and other sanctions on the longtime U.S. partner.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: As Hegseth takes charge at the Pentagon, here's what changes could be in store
item.description: There will be a lot on the plate for new Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth when he steps into his office on the Pentagon’s third floor E Ring.
item.dc:creator: Lolita C. Baldor And Tara Copp, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: 5 takeaways from Trump's whirlwind week back at the White House
item.description: President Trump stormed into the White House with a whirlwind of a first week, quickly making good on a number of campaign promises while embracing the trappings of the presidency.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: CIA releases new analysis on COVID origins favoring lab leak theory
item.description: The CIA has changed its assessment on the origins of the COVID-19 pandemic, now favoring the lab leak theory. But some worry this could be an indication of politicization.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Exclusive: White House in talks to have Oracle and U.S. investors take over TikTok
item.description: The aim is to place oversight control in the hands of American software company Oracle and other investors. Under federal law, TikTok must split apart from China, or face a nationwide ban.
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn, MPR News

item.title: Trump's DOJ asks Supreme Court to freeze cases on student debt, environment
item.description: President Trump’s Justice Department on Friday asked the Supreme Court to freeze four pending cases implicating the environment or student debt.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: He's emboldened, he's organized and he's still Trump: Takeaways from the president's opening days
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump’s first week in office isn't over yet, but already it offers signals about how his next four years in the White House may unfold.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER and JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Live updates: Trump visits Las Vegas after whirlwind first days in office
item.description: President Trump is in Las Vegas Saturday after a whirlwind first few days in the Oval Office, filled with executive orders and travel across the country as his agenda takes form in Washington.
item.dc:creator: The Hill Staff, NewsNation

item.title: This is how Trump organized his Oval Office decor
item.description: Every president gets to decorate the Oval Office to their liking — but sometimes, they keep the decor of their predecessor.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, NewsNation

item.title: Democrats and voting groups say a bid to toss out North Carolina ballots is an attack on democracy
item.description: RALEIGH, N.C. (AP) — Copland Rudolph cast a ballot in the November election, just as she has for years, with her vote counting on a long list of North Carolina contests that were settled soon after.
item.dc:creator: BY GARY D. ROBERTSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Democratic states weigh more support for immigrants as Trump administration cracks down
item.description: The resistance efforts in California, New York and other states are a counterpoint to the many Republican-led states.
item.dc:creator: DAVID A. LIEB, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump proposes 'getting rid of FEMA' while touring disaster areas
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — President Donald Trump surveyed disaster zones in California and North Carolina on Friday and said he was considering “getting rid of” the Federal Emergency Management Agency, offering the latest sign of how he is weighing sweeping changes to the nation's central organization for
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, CHRIS MEGERIAN and MAKIYA SEMINERA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hegseth confirmed as Trump's defense secretary in tie-breaking vote despite turmoil over his conduct
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Senate confirmed Pete Hegseth as the nation's defense secretary late Friday in a dramatic tie-breaking vote, swatting back questions about his qualifications to lead the Pentagon amid allegations of heavy drinking and aggressive behavior toward women.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, MARY CLARE JALONICK and FARNOUSH AMIRI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: State Department freezes new funding for nearly all US aid programs worldwide
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The State Department ordered a sweeping freeze Friday on new funding for almost all U.S. foreign assistance, making exceptions for emergency food programs and military aid to Israel and Egypt. The order threatened a quick halt to many of the billions of dollars in U.S.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER and FARNOUSH AMIRI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump orders release of JFK, RFK and MLK assassination records
item.description: President Donald Trump has ordered the release of thousands of classified governmental documents about the 1963 assassination of President John F. Kennedy, which has fueled conspiracy theories for decades.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump calls DEI programs ‘illegal’ and ‘immoral.’ Here’s how he’s ending them
item.description: President Donald Trump has issued sweeping executive actions swiftly ending diversity, equity, inclusion and accessibility programs within the federal government. Already, the work is underway.
item.dc:creator: Andrea Hsu, MPR News

item.title: NJ bill looks to eliminate hurdle for student service members
item.description: FLORHAM PARK, N.J. (PIX11) - From studying to serving: that was National Guard veteran Frank "Doc" Schupp's life in the late 2000s. He was called to deploy to Iraq at the end of his last semester of his junior year at Kean University, in the midst of prepping for finals.
item.dc:creator: Jim Vasil, PIX 11

item.title: Inauguration prayer service's pastor asks Trump to 'have mercy' on transgender kids, immigrants
item.description: “I ask you to have mercy upon the people in our country who are scared. There are gay, lesbian, transgender children, Democratic, Republican, independent families — some who fear for their lives,” she said.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Trump rolls back Biden directive to study methods of lowering prescription costs
item.description: Among the dozens of Biden-era executive orders he rescinded on day one, President Trump nixed one that aimed to explore new health care payment models that would lower prescription costs for Medicare and Medicaid beneficiaries.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, Fox 5

item.title: Trump pumps brakes on Day 1 tariffs
item.description: President Donald Trump pumped the brakes on his plan to deliver wide-ranging tariffs as soon as he took office, slow-tracking and toning down changes to the U.S. trade system that figured as a centerpiece of his campaign.
item.dc:creator: Tobias Burns, Fox 5

item.title: Supreme Court won’t take up fight over Pennsylvania mail ballot date requirement
item.description: The Supreme Court said Tuesday it won’t hear civil rights groups’ appeal seeking to invalidate Pennsylvania's dating requirement for mail ballots, which has invalidated thousands of votes each recent election and become a central legal fight over voting in the key swing state.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5

item.title: Trump attends prayer service at National Cathedral
item.description: After being sworn in on Monday, President Donald Trump marked his first full day in office with a prayer service at Washington National Cathedral on Tuesday morning.
item.dc:creator: Robert Sherman, Fox 5

item.title: Here are the biggest fights Democrats are bracing for in Trump's second term
item.description: House Democrats are bracing for major fights in the coming year as President Trump enters the White House for his second term and Republicans control all levers of power in Washington.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, Fox 5

item.title: Trump declares energy emergency
item.description: President Trump has declared an energy emergency, which his team has said would unlock additional powers to jumpstart production.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, Fox 5

item.title: Florida Republicans express optimism, state Democrat chair urges caution after inauguration
item.description: TAMPA, Fla. (WFLA) – Many Florida Republicans in the state legislature traveled to the nation’s capital for Inauguration Day events. Lawmakers like Sen. Rick Scott who works on Capitol Hill posted a video statement on X congratulating President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: NJ governor sends letter to Trump seeking to end congestion pricing
item.description: NEW JERSEY (PIX11) – New Jersey Gov. Phil Murphy sent a letter to President Donald Trump on Monday seeking to end congestion pricing in New York City. Congestion pricing went into effect on Jan. 5.
item.dc:creator: Finn Hoogensen, PIX 11

item.title: Trump to issue executive orders defining sexes, ending DEI practices in government
item.description: President-elect Trump intends to issue executive orders on his first day in office that will recognize only two sexes, male and female, and aim to end diversity, equity and inclusion (DEI) practices in the federal government, according to incoming White House officials.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5
